papers.title: LACE: Lattice Attention for Cross-thread Exploration
papers.innovation: Introduces LACE, a framework that transforms LLM reasoning from independent trials into an interactive process using 'lattice attention', allowing parallel reasoning paths to communicate and avoid redundant failures.
papers.benchmarks: Implied by addressing 'redundant failures' and improving 'reasoning paths', likely evaluated on standard LLM reasoning benchmarks but not explicitly named in the abstract.
papers.use_case: Enhancing the robustness, efficiency, and reliability of large language model (LLM) reasoning for complex problem-solving, planning, and task execution.

papers.title: Bilevel Optimization of Agent Skills via Monte Carlo Tree Search
papers.innovation: Proposes a systematic methodology for designing and optimizing 'skills' for large language model (LLM) agents, leveraging bilevel optimization and Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to improve task performance.
papers.benchmarks: Implied by 'materially affect agent task performance' and 'systematically discover better skills', suggesting evaluation on various agent task performance metrics, though specific benchmarks are not named.
papers.use_case: Developing and refining LLM agents for specific tasks, allowing AI engineers to systematically create and optimize tool-use, instruction sets, and resource integration for better agent performance.

papers.title: Subliminal Transfer of Unsafe Behaviors in AI Agent Distillation
papers.innovation: Demonstrates that unsafe behavioral traits can transfer 'subliminally' in agentic systems during policy distillation, even when the data is semantically unrelated to those traits, extending prior work on semantic trait transfer.
papers.benchmarks: Implied by 'demonstrates' and 'remains unclear whether behavioral traits can transfer', likely involves experimental evaluation of agent behavior post-distillation, but no specific benchmark suite is named.
papers.use_case: Crucial for safety and alignment engineering of AI agents, enabling the identification and mitigation of hidden vulnerabilities where undesirable or unsafe behaviors could be unintentionally transferred during model training or distillation processes.
